name: LAND AT GILESGATE DURHAM X

surface_od_m.qualifier: absent — O D LEVEL column blank on all three sheets; no Ground Level (m AOD) box on this logsheet format

units: metres, transcribed verbatim from typed logsheet — depth = base of interval below ground

intervals.verbatim: MADE GROUND - Hardcore and boulder fill.
Soft to firm brown and grey silty laminated sandy CLAY with numerous sandy partings and sand bands.
annotations: water: 'Water struck at 9.80m. Sealed off at 12.00m.' (Water Observations box, sheet 1)

intervals.verbatim: Brown and grey very silty fine to medium grained SAND.
annotations: interval spans sheets 1-2; description repeated at top of sheet 2; base 30.20 read from sheet 2 depth column

intervals.verbatim: Brown fine to medium grained slightly silty SAND with some fine to medium gravel.
annotations: interval spans sheets 2-3; description repeated at top of sheet 3; base 43.30 read from sheet 3 depth column

notes: Typed Dunelm Drilling Company logsheet (TEL 091-526-2534), 3 sheets, Borehole No BH X. Client: WELSH AND BAKER; Job No C 7567; Location: CUSSINS HOMES LAND AT GILESGATE; Date: FEBRUARY 1995; Scale 1 to 100; Boring method: CABLE PERCUSSIVE/ROTARY. Remarks (sheet 1): 'Shell and auger techniques from GL to 25.00m. Rotary techniques from 25.00m to 50.00m.' and 'Cased hole to 42.00m.' Drilling & Casing Progress column shows 13TH-16TH (February 1995 shift dates). Page 4 is a 1:1250 Development Plan (location plan) with BH X marked between Gilesgate and the Bowling Green, hand-annotated '[WOOD + WATSON]'; no strata content. Road spot height 'GILESGATE + 88.8m' appears on the plan near the site but is NOT the borehole datum and was not used. Entire hole is superficial deposits (Wear buried-valley fill: made ground, laminated clay, sands, basal boulder clay); bedrock NOT reached at 50.00 m final depth. No coal, no seam marks, no pencil marginalia. No SPT values or samples recorded on any sheet. No clerk arithmetic to cross-check (thickness derived from successive depth-column figures 1.50/18.00/30.20/43.30/50.00; all consistent).
